paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Chocolate has been consumed by humans for over 3000 years, long before it took the sweet, solid form familiar today. The earliest evidence comes from ancient Mesoamerica, where cacao pods were harvested from rainforest trees and processed into a bitter drink.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The Aztecs prized cacao beans so highly that they used them as both a beverage ingredient and a form of currency, trading them for goods across their empire. A handful of beans could buy a rabbit; a hundred could buy a slave.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: It was not until the Spanish introduced sugar in the 16th century that chocolate transformed from a bitter, spiced beverage of the elite into the sweetened drink and, eventually, solid confection that spread across Europe.

question_groups.instructions: Do the following statements agree with the information given in the passage? Write TRUE, FALSE or NOT GIVEN.

The Aztecs used cacao as a form of money.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Over the past decade, cities around the world have introduced bike-share schemes, allowing residents to rent a bicycle from one docking station and return it at another. A 2019 report found that such schemes cut short car trips within city centres by up to 12 percent.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Despite this, uptake has been uneven. Surveys consistently identify a lack of safe, separated cycle lanes as the single biggest barrier to more people cycling, ahead of cost or weather concerns.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Where cities have invested in a connected network of protected lanes, ridership has grown fastest, sometimes doubling within two years of the network's completion.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: London's scheme, launched in 2010 with 5,000 bicycles, had grown to over 11,500 bicycles and 800 docking stations by 2020, making it one of the largest in Europe.

question_groups.instructions: Complete the sentences below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.

questions.text: Bike-share schemes have reduced short __ trips in city centres.
The biggest obstacle to cycling uptake is a lack of __.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Wind turbines convert the kinetic energy of moving air into electricity. Modern turbines use large rotor blades connected to a hub; as wind pushes the blades, the hub turns a shaft connected to a generator housed inside the nacelle at the top of the tower.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Solar panels work differently, using photovoltaic cells to convert sunlight directly into electrical current. Unlike wind turbines, solar installations have no moving parts, which generally makes them cheaper to maintain over their lifetime.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: One challenge shared by both technologies is intermittency: wind and sunlight are not constantly available, so grid operators must pair renewable generation with storage or backup capacity to guarantee supply.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Battery storage costs have fallen by roughly 80 percent over the past decade, making large-scale storage increasingly viable and helping to smooth out the variability of renewable output.
